question_title: Will every party win fewer than 10 seats in the Faroe Islands’ 2026 general election before March 31, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether all parties will win fewer than 10 seats in the March 26, 2026 Faroese general election. The key evidence is a Feb 3, 2026 Spyr poll showing Party A (Fólkaflokkurin) at 37%, which would translate to ~12 seats out of 33 - clearly above 10. However, no party has EVER won 10+ seats in the modern 33-seat Løgting era (~7 elections over 25+ years). The poll is 6 weeks old and was taken before the snap election was called. Party A's 37% represents a 19.5-point surge from their 2022 result (17.5%), making it potentially unreliable. Despite the strong poll evidence pointing toward NO, the historical fragmentation pattern, age of the poll, and unprecedented nature of the result create meaningful uncertainty. Scenario analysis suggests ~27% probability that all parties stay below 10 seats.

factors.description: Feb 2026 Spyr poll shows Party A (Fólkaflokkurin) at 37%, translating to ~12 seats out of 33. This is direct polling evidence from a recognized pollster with n=500. Party A is 7 points above the ~30% threshold for 10 seats.

The poll is 6 weeks old (Feb 3) and was taken BEFORE the snap election was called (Feb 26). Pre-campaign polls in snap elections are less reliable, and campaign dynamics could meaningfully shift results.

Historical base rate: NO party has won 10+ seats in ANY of ~7 modern-era Faroese elections (33-seat Løgting). Max was 9 seats. This structural fragmentation pattern has held consistently for 25+ years.
Party A surged from 17.5% (2022) to 37% (poll) - an unprecedented 19.5-point jump. Results this far outside historical norms carry high systematic uncertainty beyond standard polling error.
Even with substantial polling error, Party A needs to drop 7+ points to stay below 10 seats. A 7-point drop from a credible poll is an unusual miss even for small electorates.
Election confirmed for March 26 and results will appear on Wikipedia before March 31 deadline. Procedural/timing conditions are virtually certain to be met.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 27% probability (YES - all parties below 10 seats)**

Key reasoning:

- **Poll evidence (strongest NO signal)**: A Feb 3, 2026 Spyr poll shows Fólkaflokkurin (People's Party) at 37%, which would translate to ~12 seats in the 33-seat Løgting — well above 10. Party A would need to drop 7+ points to below ~30% to stay under the threshold.

- **Historical pattern (strongest YES signal)**: No party has **ever** won 10+ seats in the modern 33-seat Løgting across ~7 elections spanning 25+ years. The maximum was 9 seats (Social Democrats, 2022 with 26.6%).

- **Key uncertainties**: The poll is 6 weeks old and was taken before the snap election was called on Feb 26. Party A's 37% represents an unprecedented 19.5-point surge from their 2022 result of 17.5%. Such extreme polling numbers carry higher systematic uncertainty.

- **Scenario analysis**: ~70% chance the poll roughly holds and Party A gets 10+ seats (NO); ~30% chance the poll significantly overstates Party A and historical fragmentation reasserts (YES).
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will every party win fewer than 10 seats in the Faroe Islands’ 2026 general election before March 31, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42632)
[Election Resources on the Internet: Elections to the Faroese Løgting](http://electionresources.org/dk/fo/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the final seat allocation shown for the 2026 Faroese general election on Wikipedia at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Faroese_general_election shows every party below 10 seats before March 31, 2026. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: Use Wikipedia as the primary source for the consolidated final result. If the page is incomplete or disputed, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: official Faroese election results page when posted; reputable election result trackers.
background_info:
This forecasts whether the 33-seat Løgting will be fragmented enough that no party reaches double-digit seats.
